subject: Geology
title: Arthur Holmes
description: A fast breakdown of Arthur Holmes for builders.

hook.text: Wegener said continents move but couldn't say why. Arthur Holmes found the answer: convection in the mantle.

buildup.text: Holmes proposed that heat from radioactive decay creates convection currents that drag tectonic plates.

discovery.text: He also pioneered radiometric dating, calculating Earth's age at several billion years in the 1920s.

twist.text: Like Wegener, he was ahead of his time. Full acceptance of his ideas came only after his death in 1965.

climax.text: Holmes connected radioactivity, heat, and plate motion. He gave plate tectonics its driving engine.

punchline.text: He found the engine beneath the continents.

quiz.question: What mechanism did Holmes propose for moving continents?
options: Mantle convection driven by radioactive heat
